facts.title: Parasite made Oscar history
facts.fact: Bong Joon-ho's **Parasite** (기생충) won Best Picture at the 2020 Academy Awards, becoming the **first non-English-language film** ever to take the top prize. It also won Best Director, Best Original Screenplay, and Best International Feature.
facts.note: **기생충** literally means "parasite." The director's name, 봉준호, is written family-name-first as in Korean order.

facts.title: Park Chan-wook and the Korean thriller
facts.fact: Director **Park Chan-wook** (박찬욱) is known worldwide for stylish, intense thrillers, especially **Oldboy** (올드보이), which won the Grand Prix at the 2004 Cannes Film Festival. Korean cinema has earned a global reputation for its bold thrillers.
facts.korean_en: Park Chan-wook is a famous director.
facts.note: **감독** means "director" and is also the word for a sports team's coach or manager.

facts.title: The Busan International Film Festival
facts.fact: The **Busan International Film Festival** (부산국제영화제) is one of Asia's largest film festivals. Held each autumn, it showcases new Asian and international cinema and draws filmmakers and fans from around the world.
facts.korean_en: A film festival is held in Busan.
facts.note: **영화제** combines 영화 (film) with 제 (festival); the festival is often shortened to **BIFF**.

facts.title: A nation of moviegoers
facts.fact: South Korea has one of the **highest cinema-attendance rates per person** in the world. Large multiplex theaters (영화관) fill city centers, and going to the movies is a popular outing for friends, couples, and families.
facts.korean_en: I watch a movie at the cinema.
facts.note: **영화관** literally means "film hall"; you may also hear the loanword **극장** for theater.

facts.title: Train to Busan and the zombie boom
facts.fact: The 2016 hit **Train to Busan** (부산행) is a zombie film set on a speeding train and became a worldwide success. It helped show that Korean horror and action could win huge international audiences.
facts.note: **부산행** means "bound for Busan" — 행 attached to a place name marks a train or flight's destination.

facts.title: From a small industry to a global force
facts.fact: Korean cinema dates back to the silent era of the early **1900s**, but it grew dramatically after the 1990s. Today Korean films and the directors behind them are celebrated at major festivals around the world.

vocabulary.en: to be fun, to be interesting

facts.note: **한국 영화** means "Korean film"; the boom since the late 1990s is sometimes called the Korean cinema renaissance.
